skill.name: invoice-organizer
skill.description: Organizes invoices, receipts, and financial documents by extracting key details, renaming files consistently, sorting them into folders, and creating accountant-ready summaries.
skill.summary: Organize invoices and receipts into tax-ready folders with consistent filenames and summary reports.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography alerts, but review shows these are Markdown examples, sample filenames, and non-cryptographic invoice text. The skill is documentation-only and contains no network access, credential access, prompt injection, or destructive deletion; the main risk is that it guides an agent to run local file discovery and copy or move commands after user approval.

low_findings.title: Local File Organization Commands Require User Review
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples for find, mkdir, cp, and mv to discover and organize invoice files. These commands are legitimate for the skill purpose, but they can rename, copy, or move user files, so the plan should be reviewed before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain concrete shell examples for local file discovery and file movement. They are not malicious because there is no network access, deletion, obfuscation, or user-input command construction, and the instructions request approval first.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, inline sample filenames, and example output blocks. They are not Ruby code and do not execute commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown skill document, and the flagged backticks delimit examples or inline formatted text. No Ruby runtime code or executable backtick expression is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic APIs, hashes, encryption routines, password handling, or checksum decisions. The cited lines describe invoice fields, dates, filenames, folder structures, and CSV examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation at the cited locations. The high-severity static pattern is a lexical false positive caused by ordinary invoice-organizer wording and Markdown examples.

content.value_statement: Messy invoice folders make tax preparation and expense review slow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code extract invoice details, standardize filenames, organize folders, and prepare summary reports.
actual_capabilities: Identifies invoice and receipt files in a selected folder.
Extracts dates, vendors, invoice numbers, amounts, descriptions, and payment details when visible.
Builds consistent filenames using date, vendor, document type, and description.
Suggests folder structures by vendor, category, year, quarter, or tax category.
Creates a summary CSV with invoice details and file paths.
Flags files for manual review when key details cannot be extracted.
limitations: Accuracy depends on readable document text or image quality.
It does not provide tax, accounting, or legal advice.
It should not move or rename files until the user approves the proposed plan.
It may need manual review for duplicates, split invoices, or unusual receipt formats.

use_cases.title: Prepare Records for an Accountant
use_cases.description: Sort a year of invoices into clear folders and create a summary file before sending records to an accountant.

use_cases.title: Reconcile Monthly Expenses
use_cases.description: Group receipts by category, total visible amounts, and flag documents that need a second review.

use_cases.title: Clean Email Downloads
use_cases.description: Rename generic files such as invoice.pdf and receipt.png using the vendor, date, and service description.

prompt_templates.title: Organize a Basic Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a messy receipt folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize the invoices and receipts in this folder. Show me the proposed filenames and folders before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Tax Categories
prompt_templates.prompt: Read these invoice files, extract the key details, and organize them by year and expense category. Create a summary CSV for review.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Unclear Documents
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with scans and incomplete data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Process these receipts, use filename or file dates only when document text is unclear, and place uncertain files in a Needs Review group.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Year Archive
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a large archive.
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize three years of invoices by year, category, and vendor. Detect duplicates, preserve originals, and produce a completion summary with totals.

output_examples.input: A folder with 50 mixed PDF invoices and receipt images.
output_examples.output: The assistant proposes a year and category folder plan, standardized filenames, a list of uncertain files, and a summary spreadsheet for review.

output_examples.input: Several files named invoice.pdf, invoice(1).pdf, and IMG_2847.jpg.
output_examples.output: The assistant reads visible details, suggests descriptive names with dates and vendors, and groups the files by vendor.

output_examples.input: A multi-year archive with duplicate vendor invoices.
output_examples.output: The assistant groups files by year, identifies likely duplicates, keeps originals, and lists files needing manual confirmation.

best_practices: Work on copies or keep an originals folder before approving changes.
Review the proposed organization plan before files are renamed or moved.
Use one consistent naming pattern across all years and vendors.
anti_patterns: Do not run organization commands on an entire home directory.
Do not treat extracted amounts or categories as tax advice.
Do not delete originals after organization without a separate backup.

faq.question: Can it organize scanned receipts?
faq.answer: Yes, when the image text is readable. Low-quality scans may need manual review.

faq.question: Does it change my files automatically?
faq.answer: It should show a proposed plan first. Approve the plan before renaming, copying, or moving files.

faq.question: Can it create a spreadsheet for my accountant?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create a CSV with dates, vendors, invoice numbers, amounts, categories, and file paths.

faq.question: Can it choose tax categories for me?
faq.answer: It can suggest categories from document content, but you or your accountant should verify them.

faq.question: Will it detect duplicate invoices?
faq.answer: It can compare likely duplicates and flag them, but final duplicate handling should be reviewed.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
